helpful: Provisioned full petition text (29 pp.) was the single most valuable input — the split argument, the dissental content, and the vehicle posture all come from it.
Per-Term per-fee-class grant rates in statpack.json gave a much better anchor (paid ~5.4%) than the whole-docket rate.
The docket snapshot's proceedings list made the waiver → CFR sequence legible, which is the strongest live signal on this cell.
gaps: The 'Segment base rate by salience band' statpack section described in predict.md is absent from the committed statpack, so the promised salience-band anchor could not be used.
No statpack cut for call-for-response (CFR after waiver) — it is a major cert signal, visible in the docket text, and a CFR-conditioned base rate would beat qualitative multipliers.
This case's own corpus row (e.g. its salience_score) is not directly queryable by case_id via fedcourts query, so I could not read the salience gate's own score for the case being predicted.
notes: fedcourts query has no filter that maps to 'petitions with en banc dissents' or 'petitions with N cert-stage amici'; those had to be reasoned qualitatively.
